result:
JSON is valid and every non-frozen stage is marked `changed: true`, satisfying the pivot self-report gate.

I've pre-registered the design: a redo of exp 41's domain-native contrastive-pretraining pivot (from-scratch trunk, no ImageNet/MobileNetV3, no checkpoint file), but this time with a hardened, grep-verifiable implementation brief. Exp 41 was rejected not because the science was refuted, but because its implementation left champion-backbone identifiers (`mnv3s_features8.pt`, `mobilenet_v3_small`) somewhere in `model/`. I traced this to a specific trap: `backbonecheck.py` scans docstrings verbatim (it only strips `#` comments), and the champion's `model.py` has exactly one historical prose sentence naming the old checkpoint path that a normal code rewrite wouldn't touch. I identified the exact 5 offending lines across `model/model.py` and `model/train.py`, gave the implementation agent a literal `grep` checklist plus the `backbonecheck --fingerprint` self-check to run before finishing, and wrote the trunk/pretraining-loop code in enough concrete detail (exact layer shapes, NT-Xent loss, memory-safe two-image-at-a-time contrastive sampling) to minimize ambiguity.

This is now handed off for the implementation stage to apply.

tool_input.command: cat /FROZEN 2>/dev/null || cat FROZEN 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; ls model/; echo "---library---"; sed -n '1,200p' autoresearch/library.md 2>/dev/null
